layman_headline: Crypto market is in full panic mode — patience is the best trade right now.
layman_explanation: The entire crypto market is in extreme fear territory, with Bitcoin dropping below $60,000 and spot Bitcoin ETFs on track for their worst month ever with $4 billion in outflows. Almost every altcoin is bleeding heavily over the past week. While extreme fear can eventually create buying opportunities, the selling momentum hasn't shown clear signs of exhaustion yet — catching a falling knife here is dangerous.
layman_action: Do not buy anything yet. Wait for the market to show actual stabilization — look for a day or two where Bitcoin holds a level and altcoins stop making new lows. Cash is a position, and right now it's the best one.
expert_reasoning: All swing candidates present poor risk/reward at current levels. The micro-caps (BinanceLife, GWEI, VELVET) have already pumped 34-270% and are extended. The beaten-down names (KITE, WLD, ENA) show strongly bearish chart structures with Three Black Crows and Bearish Marubozu patterns — classic falling knife territory. SOL stands out as the relative strength leader among top-20 assets with only -1.85% weekly loss versus BTC's -6.55%, but its chart bias is still -0.70 with price below all major MAs. The macro backdrop is terrible: record ETF outflows, worsening fear/greed (12, trending down from 18), and BTC dominance at 55.8% crushing altcoins. The prudent move is to wait for a capitulation wick or a clear bottoming pattern before deploying capital.
risk_factors: BTC could break below $58K support triggering cascading liquidations and another 10-15% leg down across all alts
Record ETF outflows suggest institutional selling may not be finished — this is structural, not just sentiment-driven
High BTC dominance at 55.8% means any alt position carries additional rotation risk even if BTC stabilizes
watch_list: BTC price action at $58,000-$59,000 support — a daily close below $58K would signal further downside and delay any entry
Spot BTC ETF flow data over the next 48 hours — any day of net inflows would be the first sign of institutional capitulation ending
SOL RSI dropping below 35 with a hammer or morning star candle on the daily — that would be the actionable entry signal
market_context: The crypto market is in a severe downturn with Fear & Greed at 12 and worsening, BTC below $60K, and record-breaking ETF outflows of $4 billion this month. This is a market where capital preservation takes priority over opportunity hunting.
context_banner: Extreme fear at 12/100 with record ETF outflows — the market is in capitulation territory but hasn't found a floor yet.

key_signals.description: Fear & Greed index at 12 (Extreme Fear) and worsening from 18 yesterday — sentiment has not yet reached the capitulation bounce point as it continues to deteriorate.
$4 billion in spot Bitcoin ETF outflows on track for worst month on record — institutional selling pressure is the primary driver of this downturn.
Bitcoin dipped to $59,700 as Iran de-escalation lifted stocks but NOT crypto — divergence from equities suggests crypto-specific headwinds beyond macro.
SOL RSI at 42 approaching oversold while showing relative strength (-1.85% weekly vs market -7 to -18%) — best large-cap candidate once bottom forms.
Analysts noting bullish Bitcoin RSI divergence reminiscent of 2022 bear market bottom — potential macro bottom forming but timing remains uncertain.
